findings.evidence: Lines 483 and 544 (earlier TextControl examples) include __next40pxDefaultSize. The 'final' Edit() at line 623 omits it. Verified via grep '__next40pxDefaultSize' docs/getting-started/tutorial.md — only lines 483, 544.

findings.evidence: Block-level CSS classes use wrong color values: .has-black-color uses #a156b4 (top-level strong-magenta) and .has-white-color uses #444 (top-level very-dark-grey). Input defines black=#000000, white=#ffffff.
findings.verification: Read lines 385-465 for inputs and 509-514 for outputs.
findings.fix_sketch: Use #000000 for has-black-* and #ffffff for has-white-* classes.

findings.location: line 474 (output CSS, custom property for very-dark-grey)
findings.evidence: Input line 389 defines very-dark-grey as rgb(131, 12, 8). Output line 474 shows --wp--preset--color--very-dark-grey: #444.
findings.fix_sketch: Change output to rgb(131, 12, 8).

findings.evidence: Input fontSizes (lines 407-418) only define slugs `big` and `x-large`. Output includes `.has-normal-font-size { font-size: 16; }` — no `normal` slug exists in input.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the `.has-normal-font-size` rule from the output example.

findings.evidence: Callouts say 'WordPress 5.8 supported settings/styles' but all examples use `"version": 3` (25 occurrences). version 3 requires WordPress 6.6+. A 5.8 install would not recognize version 3.
findings.fix_sketch: Update callouts to reflect WP 6.6+ context for version 3 examples.
findings.risk: Confirm intended WP version baseline for the doc before editing prose.

findings.evidence: Doc lists only `color: link`. Code APPEARANCE_TOOLS_OPT_INS also includes color: heading, button, caption; background: backgroundImage, backgroundSize, gradient; typography: lineHeight, textColumns; dimensions: aspectRatio, height, minHeight, minWidth, width; position: sticky (and experimental fixed); spacing: blockGap, margin, padding.
findings.fix_sketch: Add: color: heading, button, caption; background: gradient; typography: textColumns. (background backgroundImage/backgroundSize already in list; verify others.)
findings.risk: Check stable-vs-experimental status of position: fixed.

findings.evidence: Doc shows PostSummary implementation with Stack, PostStickyPanel, TemplateAreas. Current packages/editor/src/components/sidebar/post-summary.js uses VStack, has PostTrash, PrivatePostLastRevision, DataFormPostSummary branching, and no PostStickyPanel/TemplateAreas.

findings.evidence: Doc says SlotFills 'exported on the wp.plugins global'. PluginPostStatusInfo is exported from @wordpress/editor (packages/editor/src/components/index.js line 33), which becomes wp.editor. The plugins package has wpScript:true but doesn't export PluginPostStatusInfo.
findings.fix_sketch: Change 'wp.plugins' to 'wp.editor' (or generalize to 'from @wordpress/editor').

findings.evidence: Doc says 'component/sidebar-block-editor/sidebar-adapter.js'. Actual directory is plural 'components'. Verified packages/customize-widgets/src/components/sidebar-block-editor/sidebar-adapter.js exists.
findings.fix_sketch: Change 'component/' to 'components/' on line 23.

findings.evidence: Trailing comma after `return ... ?? item.author,` — invalid JS syntax in a return statement.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the trailing comma after the return expression.

findings.evidence: Object literal properties end with semicolons instead of commas. Confirmed via grep — at least: line 1083 (label arrow returning expression), 1937 (filterBy: false;), 1949 (isPrimary: true;), 1968/1987 (operators: [...];). These are syntax errors in JS object literals.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace trailing semicolons with commas in the affected object-literal lines.
findings.risk: Some originally-cited line numbers (1111, 1149-1152, 1528, 1693) were not confirmed by grep — fixer should scan the file for any remaining `;` separators in JS object code blocks.

findings.evidence: Line 2032 lists `email` for a second time (duplicate of line 2027); `telephone` field type is missing from the per-type table. telephone is a valid FieldTypeName (src/types/field-api.ts) with src/field-types/telephone.tsx.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace duplicate `email:` row at line 2032 with `telephone:` row using the operators from src/field-types/telephone.tsx (is, isNot, contains, notContains, startsWith, isAny, isNone, isAll).

findings.evidence: Doc imports and calls `initialize` from '@wordpress/edit-site'. Actual export is `initializeEditor` (packages/edit-site/src/index.js line 35). No `initialize` export exists.
findings.verification: grep -n 'export.*initialize' packages/edit-site/src/index.js → only initializeEditor and reinitializeEditor.

findings.evidence: Doc passes '#editor-root' (CSS selector) as id. Implementation does document.getElementById(id) (packages/edit-site/src/index.js line 36) which requires a bare id without '#'.
findings.fix_sketch: Change argument from '#editor-root' to 'editor-root'.

findings.evidence: Doc lists choices including 'composer' for `wp-env run`. The composer container was removed (validate-run-container.js line 30 throws an error if requested). The actual RUN_CONTAINERS include mysql, tests-mysql, wordpress, tests-wordpress, cli, tests-cli, phpmyadmin.
findings.fix_sketch: Update the choices list to match RUN_CONTAINERS; remove composer; update the surrounding prose about which tools are available inside containers.

findings.fix_sketch: Update choices to include tests and all.

findings.evidence: Doc says `docker ps` shows `wordpress` and `mariadb`. Actual Compose service name is `mysql` (image is `mariadb:lts`). Verified packages/env/lib/runtime/docker/build-docker-compose-config.js line 197.
findings.fix_sketch: Change `mariadb` to `mysql` in the container/service reference.

findings.evidence: Doc shows default `false`. Actual load-config.js line 125: `testsEnvironment: config.testsEnvironment !== false` — undefined evaluates to true. So default behavior is enabled.
findings.fix_sketch: Change default column to `true`.
findings.risk: Sanity-check parse-config.js for explicit default values.

findings.evidence: README says minColumnWidth is 'Mutually exclusive with columns.' But the JSDoc in packages/grid/src/dashboard-grid/types.ts explicitly documents 'Both together: columns caps the count, minColumnWidth enforces a per-tile width floor'. Implementation at dashboard-grid/index.tsx confirms via `columns !== undefined ? Math.min(columns, maxFit) : maxFit`.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace 'Mutually exclusive with columns' with the composed-behavior description from the JSDoc.
findings.risk: Consumers miss a real feature otherwise.

findings.evidence: Both components accept `renderGridOverlay` (typed in types.ts, exported as GridOverlayRenderProps from index.ts) but it's absent from both props tables.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a `renderGridOverlay` row to both tables documenting the override hook for the edit-mode overlay.

findings.evidence: Line 247 claims .eslintrc.* is a fallback. Line 253 says that fallback is deprecated. Actual code (packages/scripts/scripts/lint-js.js): `hasLintConfig` only checks flat config; if not found, it warns about legacy .eslintrc.* and uses the wp-scripts default — never the project's legacy config.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the fallback claim from line 247; clarify on line 253 that legacy .eslintrc.* files now trigger a warning and the project default is used.

findings.evidence: Doc shows runtime import `import { ThemeProvider } from '@wordpress/theme'`. src/index.ts line 3 has `export { type ThemeProvider } from './theme-provider'` — type-only export. Runtime value would be undefined.
findings.fix_sketch: Either change src/index.ts to export ThemeProvider as a value, or update README to reflect the actual (private) access path. Confirm intended public API.
findings.risk: If ThemeProvider is intentionally private, the README example itself is the wrong artifact. Fixer must verify intent.

findings.evidence: Doc lists 6 token JSONs. Repo has 7 — cursor.json exists alongside color, dimension, typography, border, elevation, motion.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a row for cursor.json describing cursor style tokens.

findings.evidence: Doc shows wpCopyFiles as array of {from,to,transform} objects. Implementation packages/wp-build/lib/build.mjs:797 destructures `{ files, transforms = {} } = packageJson.wpCopyFiles` — expects an object with a `files` glob array and a `transforms` object. Confirmed via packages/block-library/package.json usage.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace example with the real { files: [...], transforms: { php: {...} } } shape; document files and transforms sub-fields.
findings.risk: Documented wrong shape would silently no-op.

findings.evidence: Doc shows wpStyleEntryPoints as object map. Implementation packages/wp-build/lib/build.mjs:1522 treats it as an array of glob patterns (default ['src/*.scss']). Confirmed via packages/block-library/package.json.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace with an array-of-glob-patterns example; update prose accordingly.
findings.risk: Wrong shape silently disables style entry-point discovery.
